response.justification: The candidate has a broad and genuinely impressive software development background spanning web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput distributed systems, but fails to meet the most critical minimum qualification for this role: 8 years of C++ experience. The resume lists approximately 6 months of C++ work at National Instruments (late 2014 to mid 2015) and a brief mention in a bachelor thesis project, placing the candidate roughly 7.5 years short of the hard requirement. While the candidate has touched Kubernetes, AWS CDK, and micro-services handling millions of requests per second, there is no evidence of owning or architecting a cloud compute platform at the depth this role demands, and no formal technical leadership of a cloud or infrastructure engineering team.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, low-latency caching) demonstrates meaningful familiarity with performance-critical infrastructure.
Consistent knowledge-sharing and community mentorship through Refactoring Dojos, Code Kata sessions, conference talks, and public multi-day workshops signals the coaching instinct the role requires.
Polyglot pragmatism and hands-on DevOps exposure (Kubernetes, Docker, CI/CD, AWS CDK) across 15+ years of employment shows adaptability and operational awareness.
concerns: Approximately 6 months of C++ experience versus the 8-year hard minimum — the single largest and most disqualifying gap for this specific role.
No evidence of owning or designing a cloud compute platform at production scale; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is application-layer infrastructure, not platform engineering.
No formal technical leadership role managing or mentoring a team of cloud or DevOps engineers; leadership is demonstrated through community workshops rather than organizational accountability.

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (~6 months vs. 8-year minimum)
No cloud compute platform ownership or architecture
High-throughput distributed systems and DevOps exposure
